Interactive Molecular Graph with Charges

Loading visualization...

The interactive graph shows the molecular structure as a network where:

  • Nodes represent atoms (colored by partial charge)

  • Edges represent bonds (with calculated bond lengths)

  • Hover over atoms to see detailed information

  • Click and drag to rotate the 3D view

Parameter Fitting

Fitting Process (GIF)

Final Fit (PNG)

Mean Average Error (PNG)

Methods

Conformer Generation

A library of 107 low-energy conformers of capped PCY was generated using ORCA's GOAT protocol. GOAT conformer generation was run with the GFN2-XTB method. These conformers were then used as starting structures for torsion scanning and charge fitting.
QM Methods Citations:
Method: GFN2-xTB | Grimme, Stefan, DOI: doi.org/10.1021/acs.jctc.8b01176
Essential Papers:
Grimme, S., DOI: doi.org/10.1021/acs.jctc.8b01176
Neese, F., DOI: doi.org/10.1002/wcms.7019
de Souza, B., DOI: doi.org/10.1002/anie.202500393
High Priority Papers:
Grimme, S., DOI: doi.org/10.1002/wcms.1493
Suggested Additional Citations:
Neese, F., DOI: doi.org/10.1002/wcms.81
Neese, F., DOI: doi.org/10.1002/wcms.1327
Riplinger, C., DOI: doi.org/10.1063/5.0004608
Neese, F., DOI: doi.org/10.1002/wcms.1606
Optional Additional Citations:
Neese, F., DOI: doi.org/10.1016/s0009-2614(00)00662-x

Torsion Scanning

Torsion scans were performed on the set of rotatable dihedrals selected by the torsion-scan settings for PCY. Each selected torsion was scanned over 360 degrees in both forward and backward directions using 10 degree increments (37 points per direction). Relaxed scans were run at the XTB2 level with the ALPB(water) solvation model. The torsion-scanning workflow was repeated for 3 starting conformers. Only successfully completed forward/backward scan pairs were retained, and the final profile for each torsion was taken as the average over those retained scans.
QM Methods Citations:
Method: GFN2-xTB | Grimme, Stefan, DOI: doi.org/10.1021/acs.jctc.8b01176
Essential Papers:
T. E. Markland, DOI: doi.org/10.1021/acs.jpcb.3c06662
Kenneth M. Merz Jr., DOI: doi.org/10.1021/acs.jcim.3c01153
Suggested Additional Citations:
V S Pande, DOI: doi.org/10.1142/9789814295291
Travis E. Oliphant, DOI: doi.org/10.1038/s41586-020-2649-2

Charge Calculations

Partial charges for PCY were generated using the RESP2 charge-fitting protocol. Each conformer was geometry-optimized at the XTB2 level with the ALPB(water) solvation model. Two revPBE def2-SVP D3BJ evaluations were then run: one with the CPCM(water) solvation model and one in the gas phase. This workflow was applied to 4 GOAT-generated conformers of PCY. MultiWFN was used for RESP charge fitting, and conformer contributions were Boltzmann-weighted using their relative electronic energies. For RESP2, fitting was performed separately for solvated and gas-phase data, then combined as a 60:40 weighted average (solvated:gas-phase).
QM Methods Citations:
Method: GFN2-xTB | Grimme, Stefan, DOI: doi.org/10.1021/acs.jctc.8b01176
Method: revPBE | Yang, W, Title: Zhang, Y.; Yang, W. Phys. Rev. Lett., 1998, 80, 890
Method: D3BJ | Krieg, H, Title: Grimme, S.; Antony, J.; Ehrlich, S.; Krieg, H. J. Chem. Phys., 2010, 132, 154104
Essential Papers:
Neese, F., DOI: doi.org/10.1002/wcms.7019
Feiwu Chen, DOI: doi.org/10.1002/jcc.22885
Tian Lu, DOI: doi.org/10.1039/D1CP02805G
Peter A. Kollman, DOI: doi.org/10.1021/ja00074a030
Michael K. Gilson, DOI: doi.org/10.1038/s42004-020-0291-4
High Priority Papers:
Neese, F., DOI: doi.org/10.1002/jcc.10318
Krieg, H., DOI: doi.org/10.1063/1.3382344
Goerigk, L., DOI: doi.org/10.1002/jcc.21759
Neese, F., DOI: doi.org/10.1002/jcc.26942
Suggested Additional Citations:
Neese, F., DOI: doi.org/10.1002/wcms.81
Neese, F., DOI: doi.org/10.1002/wcms.1327
Riplinger, C., DOI: doi.org/10.1063/5.0004608
Neese, F., DOI: doi.org/10.1002/wcms.1606
Optional Additional Citations:
Neese, F., DOI: doi.org/10.1016/s0009-2614(00)00662-x

Parameter Fitting

MM torsion parameters were optimized iteratively to reproduce the QM torsion profiles from the scanning step. For each torsion, the fitted target was defined as: QM(torsion) = QM(total) - MM(total) + MM(torsion) where QM(total) comes from the scan data, MM(total) is computed in OpenMM on scan geometries, and MM(torsion) is reconstructed from the current torsion terms. Up to 4 cosine terms were selected from a Fourier representation of QM(torsion), and the resulting amplitudes, phases, and periodicities were written back to the parameter file. This update cycle was repeated for 12 shuffles of torsion order to reduce order-dependent bias.
QM Methods Citations:
None found.
Essential Papers:
T. E. Markland, DOI: doi.org/10.1021/acs.jpcb.3c06662
Kenneth M. Merz Jr., DOI: doi.org/10.1021/acs.jcim.3c01153
Suggested Additional Citations:
V S Pande, DOI: doi.org/10.1142/9789814295291
Travis E. Oliphant, DOI: doi.org/10.1038/s41586-020-2649-2

Time Report Summary
